calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-09-2017 Vacated

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rebecca A. Bers, of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3800, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT/CROSS DEFENDANT SAN MATEO LAND EXCHANGE DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT as to the second and third causes of action filed by cross-complainant City and County of San Francisco for equitable indemnity and equitable contribution is overruled as to both causes of action. The second and third causes of action are permissibly alleged as alternatives to the first cause of action for indemnity under Public Works Code 706 in the event that the section 706 is inapplicable to the facts and circumstances of this case. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Defendants, Cross Complainants, City And County Of San Franciscos, Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-29-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Rebecca A. Bers, of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3800, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendant/Cross-defendant San Mateo Land Exchange's motion to strike portions of the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant City and County of San Francisco is granted in part and the City is granted leave to file a first amended cross-complaint. The City acknowledges that the words "defend and" should be stricken from paragraph 13 and that current paragraph 15 should be motived to follow current paragraph 19. The City is granted leave to file a first amended cross-complaint to make that latter change. The motion to strike is denied as to the remaining portions of the cross-complaint that are sought to be stricken. The second and third causes of action are permissibly alleged as alternatives to the first cause of action for indemnity under Public Works Code 706 in the event that the section 706 is inapplicable to the facts and circumstances of this case. Moreover, the remedies sought to be stricken are not necessarily inconsistent with section 706 and the City may prove its entitlement to some or all of those remedies per separate code sections. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-27-2017 Continued To Sep-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint Against San Mateo Land Exchange
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint Against San Mateo Land Exchange is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru
